title: Scaffold Next.js dashboard project
description:
Create a new Next.js 16 dashboard app at theleak/implementation/dashboard/. This is the agentic control plane GUI.

Use the EXACT same patterns as the existing OB1 dashboard at dashboards/open-brain-dashboard-next/. Read that project's package.json, layout.tsx, globals.css, and tsconfig.json to match conventions.

Files to create:

1. package.json:
   - name: "ob1-agentic-dashboard"
   - Dependencies: next@16.2.1, react@19.2.4, react-dom@19.2.4, iron-session@^8.0.4, server-only@^0.0.1, @supabase/supabase-js@^2.49.0, recharts@^2.15.0
   - DevDeps: @tailwindcss/postcss@^4, @types/node@^20, @types/react@^19, @types/react-dom@^19, eslint@^9, eslint-config-next@16.2.1, tailwindcss@^4, typescript@^5
   - Scripts: dev, build, start, lint (same as existing dashboard)

2. tsconfig.json — Copy from existing dashboard, ensure paths alias @ -> ./

3. next.config.ts — Minimal config, same as existing dashboard

4. postcss.config.mjs — Same as existing: export default { plugins: { '@tailwindcss/postcss': {} } }

5. eslint.config.mjs — Same as existing dashboard

6. app/globals.css — COPY the exact dark theme from dashboards/open-brain-dashboard-next/app/globals.css. It uses @theme inline with these tokens: --color-bg-primary: #0a0a0f, --color-bg-surface: #111118, --color-bg-elevated: #1a1a24, --color-bg-hover: #222230, --color-border: #2a2a3a, --color-text-primary: #e8e8ef, --color-violet: #8b5cf6, etc. Add additional tokens for agent statuses: --color-agent-running: #60a5fa (blue), --color-agent-completed: #34d399 (green), --color-agent-failed: #f87171 (red), --color-agent-pending: #9ca3af (gray), --color-agent-skipped: #fbbf24 (yellow).

7. app/layout.tsx — Root layout matching existing pattern: Geist + Geist_Mono fonts, html with font variables and h-full antialiased, body with min-h-screen flex bg-bg-primary text-text-primary, Sidebar component, main with flex-1 ml-56 min-h-screen, children in max-w-7xl mx-auto px-8 py-8.

8. .env.example:
   NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://YOUR-PROJECT-REF.supabase.co
   NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key
   OB1_ACCESS_KEY=your-service-role-key
   SESSION_SECRET=generate-with-openssl-rand-hex-32

9. .gitignore — Standard Next.js gitignore (node_modules, .next, .env, etc.)

10. middleware.ts — Copy auth middleware pattern from existing dashboard (redirect unauthenticated users to /login)

After creating all files, run: cd theleak/implementation/dashboard && npm install

Verify the project builds: npm run build

Do NOT create any page files beyond layout.tsx — other tasks handle those.

title: Build API client library and type definitions
description:
Create the API client library for the agentic dashboard at theleak/implementation/dashboard/.

The backend uses 7 Supabase Edge Functions with a unified envelope protocol. Read theleak/implementation/runtime/src/ob1-client.ts to understand all 52 API actions and their signatures. The Edge Function envelope is:

  POST /functions/v1/{functionName}
  Headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer KEY', apikey: KEY, Content-Type: 'application/json' }
  Body: { action: string, params: Record<string, any> }
  Response: { data?: any, error?: string }

Create these files:

1. lib/types.ts — TypeScript interfaces mirroring theleak/implementation/runtime/src/types.ts. Include:
   - PermissionMode enum, ToolSpec, PermissionPolicy, AuditEntry, AuditSummary
   - SessionState, WorkflowStep, RecoveryResult, BudgetStatus, BudgetConfig, UsageEntry
   - SystemEvent (with category: 'boot'|'session'|'coordinator'|'tool'|'hook'|'budget'|'memory' and severity: 'info'|'warn'|'error'|'fatal')
   - VerificationResult, DoctorCheckResult, BootRun
   - AgentType, AgentRun, AgentRunStatus ('pending'|'running'|'completed'|'failed'|'cancelled'), AgentMessage, AgentSummary
   - MemoryResult (thought_id, content, metadata, similarity, created_at)
   - SkillDef, HookConfig, HookEvent, Plugin
   - NightTask, TaskResult, NightRunReport (from night-runner.ts)

2. lib/ob1-api.ts — Server-side API client (import 'server-only'). Model it on dashboards/open-brain-dashboard-next/lib/api.ts but use the Edge Function envelope pattern. Include:

   class OB1ApiError extends Error { constructor(message, functionName, action, status) }

   function ob1Call<T>(accessKey, functionName, action, params): Promise<T>
     — builds URL from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL + '/functions/v1/' + functionName
     — sends POST with auth headers and { action, params } body
     — throws OB1ApiError on !res.ok or body.error
     — returns body.data ?? body

   Export wrapper functions for all major actions:
   - listTools(key, filters?), getPolicies(key), setPolicy(key, policy), getAuditSummary(key, sessionId)
   - createSession(key, config?), getSession(key, sessionId), updateSession(key, sessionId, updates), checkBudget(key, sessionId), recordUsage(key, sessionId, usage)
   - logEvent(key, event), queryEvents(key, filters), runVerification(key, params)
   - runDoctor(key), recordBoot(key, bootRun), getConfig(key, scope?)
   - memoryStore(key, content, metadata), memoryRecall(key, query, filters?), memoryForget(key, thoughtId, reason), memoryUpdate(key, thoughtId, newContent)
   - listSkills(key, filters?), listHooks(key, filters?), listPlugins(key, filters?)
   - listAgentTypes(key), spawnAgent(key, type, options), updateAgentStatus(key, runId, status, result?), getAgentRun(key, runId), listAgentRuns(key, filters?), sendMessage(key, from, to, content, options?), getMessages(key, runId, undeliveredOnly?), getAgentSummary(key, coordinatorId)

3. lib/supabase.ts — Supabase client for Realtime:
   import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
   export const supabase = createClient(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!)
   Mark this as client-safe (no 'server-only' import).

4. lib/auth.ts — Copy the iron-session pattern from dashboards/open-brain-dashboard-next/lib/auth.ts. Session stores: apiKey (the OB1 access key). Provide: getSession(), requireSessionOrRedirect().

5. lib/format.ts — Utility functions:
   - formatUSD(cents_or_dollars: number): string — '$1.23'
   - formatDuration(ms: number): string — '2m 34s' or '1h 12m'
   - formatRelativeTime(iso: string): string — '3 minutes ago'
   - formatDateTime(iso: string): string — 'Apr 3, 2026, 10:30 PM'
   - truncate(str: string, maxLen: number): string
   - statusColor(status: string): string — returns Tailwind color class

title: Build shared UI components and Sidebar
description:
Create shared UI components for the agentic dashboard at theleak/implementation/dashboard/components/.

Match the visual style of dashboards/open-brain-dashboard-next/ exactly. Read that project's Sidebar.tsx, ThoughtCard.tsx, StatsWidget.tsx for reference. All components use the dark theme CSS tokens defined in globals.css.

Create these components:

1. components/Sidebar.tsx — 'use client' component. Navigation sidebar with these items:
   - Dashboard (/) — grid icon
   - Agents (/agents) — robot/cpu icon
   - Tasks (/tasks) — checklist icon
   - Memory (/memory) — brain icon
   - Sessions (/sessions) — chat icon
   - Morning Report (/morning) — sun icon
   - Health (/health) — heart/pulse icon
   - Tools (/tools) — wrench icon
   Match the EXACT pattern from the existing dashboard Sidebar.tsx:
   - Fixed left, h-screen, w-56, bg-bg-surface, border-r border-border
   - OB logo at top (w-8 h-8 rounded-lg bg-violet, 'OB' text)
   - Nav items with SVG icons, active state uses bg-violet-surface text-violet border border-violet/20
   - Sign out button at bottom
   Create inline SVG icon components for each nav item (same pattern as existing: simple 18x18 SVGs).

2. components/StatusBadge.tsx — Props: { status: string, pulse?: boolean }
   Renders a small badge with colored dot + text.
   Colors: running=agent-running (blue, with pulse animation if pulse=true), completed=agent-completed (green), failed=agent-failed (red), pending=agent-pending (gray), skipped=agent-skipped (yellow), active=success, paused=warning.
   Tailwind classes only, no inline styles.

3. components/BudgetGauge.tsx — Props: { spent: number, limit: number, label?: string }
   Visual budget indicator using a horizontal bar:
   - Background: bg-bg-elevated
   - Fill: green (<50%), yellow (50-80%), red (>80%)
   - Text showing '$X.XX / $Y.YY' and percentage
   - Optional label above
   Pure Tailwind, no chart library needed for this simple version.

4. components/Card.tsx — Props: { children, className?, title?, subtitle?, actions? }
   Reusable container: bg-bg-surface border border-border rounded-lg p-5.
   Optional title (text-lg font-medium) and subtitle (text-sm text-text-secondary).
   Optional actions slot in the top-right corner.

5. components/EmptyState.tsx — Props: { icon?: ReactNode, title: string, description?: string, action?: ReactNode }
   Centered content with muted styling for empty lists/tables.

6. components/LoadingSpinner.tsx — Two variants:
   - Spinner: animated SVG spinner with violet color
   - Skeleton: pulsing bg-bg-elevated rounded blocks (like LoadingCard, LoadingTable)

7. components/Toast.tsx — 'use client' component with ToastProvider and useToast hook:
   - Types: success (green), error (red), info (blue), warning (yellow)
   - Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds
   - Stack in bottom-right corner
   - Slide-in animation
   Wrap the app with ToastProvider in layout.tsx.

8. components/PageHeader.tsx — Props: { title: string, subtitle?: string, actions?: ReactNode }
   Consistent page header: h1 text-2xl font-semibold, optional subtitle text-text-secondary text-sm, actions aligned right.

9. components/DataTable.tsx — Generic table component:
   Props: { columns: Column[], data: T[], sortable?: boolean, onSort?, emptyMessage? }
   Column: { key: string, label: string, render?: (value, row) => ReactNode, sortable?: boolean }
   Dark-themed table with hover rows, header styling.

10. app/login/page.tsx + app/login/layout.tsx + app/login/LoginForm.tsx — Copy the login pattern from the existing dashboard. Login form accepts an API key, validates against the OB1 API (call runDoctor or a simple health check), stores in iron-session.
    app/api/logout/route.ts — Clears session.

Do NOT create any page components beyond login — other tasks handle those.

title: Build Dashboard Home page (/)
description:
Build the main dashboard home page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/page.tsx.

This is the system overview — the first thing Robin sees after login. It should feel like a mission control screen.

Read the existing dashboard page at dashboards/open-brain-dashboard-next/app/page.tsx for the pattern (server component, requireSessionOrRedirect, parallel data fetching with Promise.all, error boundary with try/catch).

Page structure (app/page.tsx — server component):

1. Data fetching — use requireSessionOrRedirect() to get apiKey, then Promise.all:
   - listAgentRuns(apiKey, { limit: 50 }) — count by status
   - queryEvents(apiKey, { limit: 20 }) — recent activity
   - runDoctor(apiKey) — quick health snapshot

2. Status Strip — 4 cards in a grid (grid-cols-4 gap-4):
   - 'Agents Running' — count of runs with status 'running', blue bg accent
   - 'Completed Today' — count of runs completed in last 24h, green bg accent
   - 'Failed' — count of failed runs, red bg accent (0 = green)
   - 'Health' — doctor pass/warn/fail summary, color based on worst status
   Each card: Card component with large number (text-3xl font-bold), label below, colored left border or top accent.

3. Budget Gauge — BudgetGauge component showing total USD spent across all sessions vs configured limit. If no budget data available, show a placeholder.

4. Recent Activity Feed — List of system events:
   - Each event: timestamp (formatRelativeTime), category badge, severity indicator (colored dot), title, truncated detail
   - Max 20 items, scrollable if needed
   - Link to /health for full event log

5. Quick Actions — Row of buttons:
   - 'Run Doctor' — client component button that POST to /api/actions/doctor, shows results in expandable section
   - 'New Memory' — link to /memory with create=true query param
   - 'View Morning Report' — link to /morning

Also create these API routes:

- app/api/dashboard/route.ts — GET handler that aggregates all dashboard data (agent counts, events, health) into one response. Uses requireSessionOrRedirect internally.

- app/api/actions/doctor/route.ts — POST handler that calls runDoctor(apiKey) and returns results.

- app/api/actions/spawn/route.ts — POST handler that accepts { agent_type, task_prompt, budget } and calls spawnAgent().

Style: Use Card, StatusBadge, BudgetGauge, PageHeader components from task-003. Follow the dark theme.

Error handling: Wrap data fetch in try/catch, show error state with bg-danger/10 border-danger/30 message (same pattern as existing dashboard).

title: Build Agent Monitor page (/agents)
description:
Build the real-time agent monitoring page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/agents/page.tsx.

This page shows all agent runs and allows spawning new agents. It's the operational core for watching autonomous agents work.

Files to create:

1. app/agents/page.tsx — Server component that fetches initial data:
   - listAgentRuns(apiKey, { limit: 50 }) — all recent runs
   - listAgentTypes(apiKey) — available agent types for spawn modal
   Pass data to client component AgentMonitor.

2. app/agents/AgentMonitor.tsx — 'use client' component. Main interactive view:

   a) Header: PageHeader with title 'Agent Monitor', subtitle showing counts ('3 running, 12 completed, 1 failed'), action button 'Spawn Agent' that opens spawn modal.

   b) Filter bar: Status filter buttons (All, Running, Completed, Failed, Pending). Type filter dropdown.

   c) Agent cards list: For each agent run, render a card:
      - Left: StatusBadge (with pulse animation for running)
      - Center: agent type badge (small pill), task prompt (truncated to 100 chars), run ID (monospace, truncated)
      - Right: duration (live-updating for running agents using setInterval), cost ($X.XX), token count
      - Bottom: expand/collapse toggle
      - Expanded section: full task prompt, output_summary, error_message (in red bg), messages list

   d) Live updates: Import supabase from lib/supabase.ts. Subscribe to agent_runs table:
      ```
      supabase.channel('agent-runs-live')
        .on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'agent_runs' }, payload => {
          // Update the matching run in local state, or add if INSERT
        })
        .subscribe()
      ```
      Clean up subscription on unmount.

3. app/agents/SpawnModal.tsx — 'use client' modal component:
   - Overlay with backdrop blur
   - Form fields: Agent Type (dropdown from agentTypes prop), Task Prompt (textarea, required), Budget Limit USD (number input, default 2.00), Max Turns (number input, default 20)
   - Submit calls POST /api/agents with { agent_type, task_prompt, budget_config: { max_usd }, max_turns }
   - Shows success toast on spawn, error toast on failure
   - Closes modal on success

4. app/api/agents/route.ts:
   - GET: calls listAgentRuns(apiKey, filters from query params) and returns JSON
   - POST: calls spawnAgent(apiKey, body.agent_type, { task_prompt, budget_config, ... }) and returns the new run

5. app/api/agents/[id]/route.ts:
   - GET: calls getAgentRun(apiKey, id) and getMessages(apiKey, id) and returns both

6. app/api/agents/types/route.ts:
   - GET: calls listAgentTypes(apiKey) and returns JSON

Styling: Cards use bg-bg-surface with hover:bg-bg-hover transition. Running agents have a subtle blue left border (border-l-2 border-agent-running). Failed agents have red left border. Use StatusBadge for status display.

title: Build Task Manager page (/tasks)
description:
Build the task management page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/tasks/page.tsx.

This page is where Robin creates and prioritizes tasks for overnight agent runs. Tasks are stored as OB1 memories (thoughts) with specific tags, matching the NightRunner's loadTasksFromThoughts() format.

Task storage format (matching night-runner.ts):
- Stored via memoryStore(content=task_description, metadata={ type: 'task', status: 'pending', title, priority, agent_type, depends_on, max_turns, max_usd, tags: ['task', 'night-run'] })
- Retrieved via memoryRecall('pending tasks to execute', { tags: ['task', 'night-run'], limit: 100 })

Files to create:

1. app/tasks/page.tsx — Server component:
   - Fetch tasks: memoryRecall(apiKey, 'pending tasks', { tags: ['task', 'night-run'], limit: 100 })
   - Fetch agent types: listAgentTypes(apiKey)
   - Pass to TaskManager client component

2. app/tasks/TaskManager.tsx — 'use client' component:

   a) Header: PageHeader 'Task Manager' with two action buttons: 'New Task' and 'Start Night Run'

   b) Task list: Ordered by priority (ascending). Each task card shows:
      - Drag handle (hamburger icon) on left for reordering
      - Priority number in a circle
      - Title (bold)
      - Description preview (truncated, text-text-secondary)
      - Agent type badge (small pill)
      - Cost estimate badge ('$X.XX max')
      - Dependency indicators (if depends_on has items, show 'Depends on: task-001, task-002')
      - StatusBadge
      - Edit button (pencil icon)
      - Delete button (trash icon, with confirmation)

   c) Drag-to-reorder: Use native HTML5 drag-and-drop (onDragStart, onDragOver, onDrop). When dropped, recalculate priority numbers (1, 2, 3...) and batch-update via POST /api/tasks/reorder.

   d) Inline task count summary above list: 'X tasks, estimated $Y.YY total'

3. app/tasks/TaskEditor.tsx — Modal form for creating/editing tasks:
   - Title (text input, required)
   - Description (textarea, 6 rows, required. Hint: 'Be specific — this will be executed by an autonomous agent')
   - Priority (number 1-10, default 5)
   - Agent Type (dropdown populated from agentTypes, default 'general_purpose')
   - Dependencies (multi-select checkboxes of existing task IDs/titles)
   - Max Turns (number, default 20)
   - Budget Cap USD (number, default 2.00)
   - Save button: POST /api/tasks (create) or PUT /api/tasks/[id] (edit)
   - Cancel button

4. app/tasks/NightRunConfig.tsx — Collapsible panel for night run configuration:
   - Total Budget USD (number input, default 20)
   - Max Duration Hours (number input, default 8)
   - Max Concurrent Agents (number input, default 3)
   - 'Start Night Run' button with confirmation dialog ('This will start autonomous execution. Are you sure?')
   - On confirm: POST /api/night-run with config

5. API routes:
   - app/api/tasks/route.ts — GET: memoryRecall for tasks; POST: memoryStore new task
   - app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts — PUT: memoryUpdate; DELETE: memoryForget with reason 'Deleted from task manager'
   - app/api/tasks/reorder/route.ts — POST: accepts array of { thought_id, priority }, batch updates via memoryUpdate
   - app/api/night-run/route.ts — POST: This triggers the night runner. For now, store a 'night-run-config' thought with the config, and show a success message explaining the user should start the NightRunner CLI pointing at their tasks.

Styling: Task cards use Card component. Drag target highlights with border-dashed border-violet/30. Priority circles use bg-violet text-white for high priority (1-3), bg-bg-elevated text-text-secondary for low priority.

title: Build Memory Explorer page (/memory)
description:
Build the memory explorer page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/memory/page.tsx.

This page lets Robin browse and search the OB1 memory system (the thoughts table via the agent-memory Edge Function).

Files to create:

1. app/memory/page.tsx — Server component:
   - Initial load: memoryRecall(apiKey, '', { limit: 20 }) to get recent memories
   - Pass to MemoryExplorer client component

2. app/memory/MemoryExplorer.tsx — 'use client' main component:

   a) Search bar (prominent, at top):
      - Text input with search icon, placeholder 'Search memories semantically...'
      - Filter toggles below: Scope (all/user/agent/system), Type dropdown (fact/preference/procedure/experience/reflection)
      - Similarity threshold slider (0.5 to 1.0, default 0.7)
      - Search triggers on Enter or after 500ms debounce
      - Calls POST /api/memory/search with { query, filters }

   b) Results grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-4):
      - Each memory card (MemoryCard component):
        - Content preview (first 200 chars, text-sm)
        - Scope badge: user=info color, agent=violet, system=text-muted
        - Type badge
        - Similarity score bar (if search result): thin bar from 0-100% with percentage label
        - Tags as small pills
        - Created date (formatRelativeTime)
        - Click to expand

   c) Expanded memory view (modal or drawer):
      - Full content (rendered with whitespace-pre-wrap)
      - All metadata as key-value pairs
      - Edit button -> opens editor with textarea pre-filled with content
      - Forget button -> confirmation with reason input, calls DELETE /api/memory/[id]
      - Close button

   d) Create Memory floating button (bottom-right, fixed position):
      - '+' icon button with bg-violet
      - Opens CreateMemoryModal

3. app/memory/CreateMemoryModal.tsx — Modal form:
   - Content (textarea, 8 rows, required)
   - Scope selector (radio: user/agent/system, default 'user')
   - Type selector (dropdown: fact/preference/procedure/experience/reflection, default 'fact')
   - Tags (text input, comma-separated)
   - Importance (range slider 1-10, default 5)
   - Store button: POST /api/memory with { content, metadata: { memory_scope, memory_type, tags, importance } }

4. app/memory/MemoryCard.tsx — Reusable card for displaying a memory result.

5. API routes:
   - app/api/memory/route.ts — GET: memoryRecall(apiKey, '', { limit: 20 }); POST: memoryStore(apiKey, body.content, body.metadata)
   - app/api/memory/[id]/route.ts — PUT: memoryUpdate(apiKey, id, body.content); DELETE: memoryForget(apiKey, id, body.reason)
   - app/api/memory/search/route.ts — POST: memoryRecall(apiKey, body.query, body.filters)

Styling: Memory cards use bg-bg-surface with content in text-sm. Scope badges use the StatusBadge component with custom colors. The search bar should be visually prominent with a slightly elevated background (bg-bg-elevated) and larger text.

title: Build Session History page (/sessions)
description:
Build the session history page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/sessions/page.tsx.

This page shows past conversation sessions with their budgets, durations, and event logs.

Files to create:

1. app/sessions/page.tsx — Server component:
   - Fetch recent events to derive session list: queryEvents(apiKey, { category: 'session', limit: 100 })
   - Group events by session_id to build session summaries
   - Pass to SessionList client component

2. app/sessions/SessionList.tsx — 'use client' component:

   a) Header: PageHeader 'Session History'

   b) Filter bar: Status filter (all/active/completed/failed/paused), date range (today/week/month/all)

   c) Session table using DataTable component:
      - Columns: Session ID (truncated, monospace), Status (StatusBadge), Started, Duration (formatDuration), Agent Type, Actions
      - Sortable by started date and duration
      - Click row to expand

   d) Expanded session detail:
      - Full session ID (copyable)
      - Session metadata
      - Budget breakdown: if available from checkBudget, show tokens used, USD spent, limits
      - Event timeline: events for this session from queryEvents({ session_id }), rendered as a vertical timeline with severity-colored dots
      - 'View Events' link to /health with session filter pre-set

3. API routes:
   - app/api/sessions/route.ts — GET: queries events to derive session list. Groups by session_id, computes duration and status from event sequences.
   - app/api/sessions/[id]/route.ts — GET: calls getSession(apiKey, id) (may fail if session doesn't exist in state table), queryEvents(apiKey, { session_id: id, limit: 50 }), and optionally checkBudget(apiKey, id). Returns all data.

Styling: Use DataTable for the main list. Expanded details use a bordered section below the row. Event timeline uses a vertical line with colored dots.

Note: Session data may be sparse since this is a new system. Handle empty state gracefully with EmptyState component showing 'No sessions yet. Sessions are created when agents run.'

title: Build Morning Report page (/morning)
description:
Build the morning report page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/morning/page.tsx.

This is the MOST IMPORTANT page — it's what Robin sees first when he wakes up. Design it for a sleepy human who needs to quickly understand what happened overnight.

The NightRunner (theleak/implementation/runtime/src/night-runner.ts) generates a NightRunReport and stores it as an OB1 memory with tags ['night-run-report'] and type 'report'. The report JSON structure is:

  {
    started_at: string,
    completed_at: string,
    duration_minutes: number,
    total_tasks: number,
    completed_tasks: number,
    failed_tasks: number,
    skipped_tasks: number,
    total_usd_spent: number,
    total_tokens_used: number,
    task_results: [{ task_id, title, status, agent_type, started_at, completed_at, duration_ms, usd_spent, tokens_used, output_summary, error }],
    errors: string[]
  }

Files to create:

1. app/morning/page.tsx — Server component:
   - Fetch latest report: memoryRecall(apiKey, 'night run report', { tags: ['night-run-report'], limit: 1 })
   - Parse the content as JSON (it's stored as a JSON string in the thought content)
   - If no report found, show a friendly empty state
   - Pass parsed NightRunReport to MorningReport client component

2. app/morning/MorningReport.tsx — Main report view:

   a) Hero section (large, centered):
      - Time-appropriate greeting: 'Good morning' (5-12), 'Good afternoon' (12-17), 'Good evening' (17+)
      - One-line summary: '{completed}/{total} tasks completed, ${usd_spent} spent'
      - Overall status icon: green checkmark (all completed), yellow warning (some failed/skipped), red alert (all failed)
      - Night run duration: 'Ran for X hours Y minutes'

   b) Task Results — Clean, scannable list:
      - Each task as a row/card:
        - Status icon (checkmark/X/skip) with color
        - Task title (bold)
        - Duration (formatDuration)
        - Cost ($X.XX)
        - One-line output_summary (text-text-secondary)
        - If failed: error message in red-tinted section
      - Sort: completed first, then failed, then skipped

   c) Budget Breakdown — recharts BarChart:
      - X-axis: task titles (truncated)
      - Y-axis: USD spent
      - Bars colored by status (green=completed, red=failed, yellow=skipped)
      - Horizontal reference line at total budget limit
      - Total spent annotation

   d) Attention Required — Only shown if errors.length > 0 or failed_tasks > 0:
      - Red/orange bordered section
      - List of errors
      - Failed task details with suggested actions ('Retry this task?')
      - Skipped tasks with reason

   e) Quick Actions:
      - 'Plan Next Night' -> link to /tasks
      - 'View Agent Details' -> link to /agents
      - 'Check Health' -> link to /health

3. app/morning/ReportHistory.tsx — Optional section below main report:
   - 'Previous Reports' expandable section
   - Fetches memoryRecall(apiKey, 'night run report', { tags: ['night-run-report'], limit: 10 })
   - Shows date, task count, success rate, cost for each past report
   - Click to load full report

4. API routes:
   - app/api/morning/route.ts — GET: fetch latest report from memory
   - app/api/morning/history/route.ts — GET: fetch last 10 reports

Styling: This page should feel CALM and informational, not alarming (unless there are actual errors). Use generous whitespace, large text for the hero, muted colors for secondary info. The hero should be visually distinct — maybe a subtle gradient from bg-bg-primary to bg-bg-surface.

Mobile: This page MUST look good on a phone. Robin will check it from bed. Stack everything vertically, ensure touch targets are large enough.

title: Build System Health page (/health)
description:
Build the system health monitoring page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/health/page.tsx.

This page shows Doctor results, boot history, and the system event log.

The Doctor system (theleak/implementation/runtime/src/doctor.ts) runs checks in 6 categories: credentials, connections, tables, functions, config, memory. Each check returns { name, category, status: 'pass'|'warn'|'fail', detail, duration_ms }.

Files to create:

1. app/health/page.tsx — Server component:
   - Fetch doctor results: runDoctor(apiKey)
   - Fetch recent events: queryEvents(apiKey, { limit: 50 })
   - Pass to HealthDashboard client component

2. app/health/HealthDashboard.tsx — 'use client' component:

   a) Doctor Results section:
      - 6 category cards in a grid (grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 gap-4)
      - Each card shows: category name, icon, overall status (worst check in category)
      - Expand to see individual checks: check name, status badge, detail, duration
      - 'Re-run Doctor' button that POST to /api/health/doctor, replaces results in-place
      - Summary strip at top: 'X pass, Y warn, Z fail' with colored counts

   b) Event Log section:
      - Filter bar: severity dropdown (all/info/warn/error/fatal), category dropdown, session ID text input
      - Event list (virtual scroll or paginated, max 50 at a time):
        - Each event: timestamp (formatDateTime), severity icon (info=blue dot, warn=yellow triangle, error=red circle, fatal=red pulse), category badge, title, detail (expandable)
      - Auto-refresh toggle (polls every 10s when on)
      - 'Load More' button for pagination

   c) Optional: Boot History section:
      - If boot data available, show last 5 boot runs
      - Boot ID, started at, duration, status, failed phase

3. API routes:
   - app/api/health/doctor/route.ts — GET: runDoctor(apiKey); POST: run specific category checks
   - app/api/health/events/route.ts — GET: queryEvents(apiKey, filters from query params)

Styling: Doctor cards use bg-bg-surface with a colored top border based on worst status (green=pass, yellow=warn, red=fail). Event severity colors match the theme: info=info, warn=warning, error=danger, fatal=danger with pulse.

title: Build Tool Registry page (/tools)
description:
Build the tool registry and permissions page at theleak/implementation/dashboard/app/tools/page.tsx.

This page shows all registered tools, their permissions, and the audit trail.

Files to create:

1. app/tools/page.tsx — Server component:
   - Fetch tools: listTools(apiKey)
   - Fetch policies: getPolicies(apiKey)
   - Pass to ToolRegistry client component

2. app/tools/ToolRegistry.tsx — 'use client' component with two tabs: 'Tools' and 'Policies'

   a) Tools tab:
      - Filter bar: Source type (all/built_in/plugin/skill/mcp), search text input
      - Tool list using DataTable:
        - Columns: Name (monospace), Source Type (badge), Permission Level (badge with color: read_only=green, workspace_write=yellow, danger_full_access=red, prompt=blue, allow=purple), Side Effects (icon row), Enabled (toggle)
        - Side effect icons: file write (doc icon), network (globe icon), destructive (warning icon), spawns process (terminal icon). Only show icons for true values.
      - Click row to expand tool detail:
        - Full description
        - Input schema (rendered as formatted JSON in a code block with bg-bg-elevated)
        - All side effect profile fields
        - Aliases list
        - MCP server URL (if mcp source)
        - Metadata (formatted JSON)

   b) Policies tab:
      - Policy cards (grid layout):
        - Policy name (bold)
        - Description
        - Active mode badge
        - Handler type badge (interactive/coordinator/swarm_worker)
        - Tool overrides count
        - Deny tools count
        - Edit button -> opens PolicyEditor
      - 'Create Policy' button

3. app/tools/PolicyEditor.tsx — Modal form:
   - Name (text input)
   - Description (textarea)
   - Active Mode (dropdown: read_only, workspace_write, danger_full_access, prompt, allow)
   - Handler Type (dropdown: interactive, coordinator, swarm_worker)
   - Tool Overrides (dynamic key-value list: tool name -> permission mode)
   - Deny Tools (tag input)
   - Allow Tools (tag input)
   - Save button: POST /api/tools/policies

4. API routes:
   - app/api/tools/route.ts — GET: listTools(apiKey)
   - app/api/tools/policies/route.ts — GET: getPolicies(apiKey); POST: setPolicy(apiKey, body)
   - app/api/tools/audit/route.ts — GET: getAuditSummary(apiKey, query.session_id)

Styling: Tool names in monospace. Permission levels color-coded. Side effect icons are small, muted by default, colored when active. JSON code blocks use bg-bg-elevated with text-sm font-mono.

title: Wire up Realtime subscriptions and notifications
Make dashboard responsive for mobile
description:
Make the agentic dashboard at theleak/implementation/dashboard/ responsive for mobile devices, especially the morning report page.

Robin checks the morning report from his phone in bed. It must be usable on a 390px-wide screen.

Files to modify:

1. components/Sidebar.tsx — Make collapsible on mobile:
   - Add state: const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false)
   - Below md breakpoint: sidebar is hidden by default (translate-x-[-100%])
   - Hamburger button appears in a fixed top-left corner (visible only below md)
   - When open: overlay backdrop with bg-black/50, sidebar slides in from left
   - Auto-close when a nav link is clicked
   - Transition: transform 200ms ease-in-out

2. app/layout.tsx — Adjust main content:
   - Change ml-56 to md:ml-56 (no left margin on mobile since sidebar overlays)
   - Add a top padding on mobile for the hamburger button area: pt-14 md:pt-0

3. app/morning/MorningReport.tsx — Mobile-optimized:
   - Hero section: stack vertically, reduce text sizes (text-xl on mobile, text-3xl on desktop)
   - Task results: switch from table to card layout on mobile (grid-cols-1)
   - Budget chart: set maxWidth to 100%, ensure it doesn't overflow
   - Attention section: full-width, prominent
   - Increase touch targets: all buttons min-h-11 (44px)

4. app/page.tsx (Dashboard) — Responsive grid:
   - Status strip: grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4
   - Activity feed: full width on all sizes
   - Quick actions: flex-wrap on mobile

5. All modals (SpawnModal, TaskEditor, CreateMemoryModal, PolicyEditor):
   - On mobile (below md): become full-screen drawers instead of centered modals
   - Slide up from bottom
   - Full width, rounded-t-xl at top

6. DataTable component:
   - Add horizontal scroll wrapper on mobile: overflow-x-auto
   - Optionally: hide less-important columns below certain breakpoints

7. General responsive fixes:
   - Ensure no horizontal overflow on any page
   - Text truncation works on mobile (truncate class on long content)
   - Padding: px-4 on mobile, px-8 on desktop

Test by resizing browser to 390px width and checking all pages render without horizontal scroll or overlapping elements.

title: Final integration test and build verification
description:
Run a comprehensive integration test and build verification on the agentic dashboard at theleak/implementation/dashboard/.

This is the LAST task. When it passes, Robin wakes up to a working dashboard.

Steps:

1. Build verification:
   - cd theleak/implementation/dashboard
   - Run: npm run build
   - If build fails: read error messages, fix TypeScript errors, missing imports, or broken references. Common issues:
     - Missing type imports between lib/types.ts and components
     - Import paths using wrong alias
     - Server components importing 'use client' modules incorrectly
     - Missing 'export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"' on pages that fetch data
   - Re-run build until it succeeds with zero errors.

2. Lint check:
   - Run: npm run lint
   - Fix any lint errors (unused variables, missing deps in useEffect, etc.)

3. Page route verification — Ensure these files exist and export default components:
   - app/page.tsx (Dashboard)
   - app/agents/page.tsx (Agent Monitor)
   - app/tasks/page.tsx (Task Manager)
   - app/memory/page.tsx (Memory Explorer)
   - app/sessions/page.tsx (Session History)
   - app/morning/page.tsx (Morning Report)
   - app/health/page.tsx (System Health)
   - app/tools/page.tsx (Tool Registry)
   - app/login/page.tsx (Login)

4. API route verification — Ensure these files exist and export handlers:
   - app/api/dashboard/route.ts
   - app/api/agents/route.ts
   - app/api/agents/[id]/route.ts
   - app/api/agents/types/route.ts
   - app/api/tasks/route.ts
   - app/api/tasks/[id]/route.ts
   - app/api/memory/route.ts
   - app/api/memory/[id]/route.ts
   - app/api/memory/search/route.ts
   - app/api/sessions/route.ts
   - app/api/sessions/[id]/route.ts
   - app/api/morning/route.ts
   - app/api/health/doctor/route.ts
   - app/api/health/events/route.ts
   - app/api/tools/route.ts
   - app/api/tools/policies/route.ts
   - app/api/logout/route.ts

5. Seed mock data (if database is empty) — Create a script at scripts/seed-mock-data.ts that uses the API client to create:
   - 5 sample memories (one fact, one preference, one procedure, one experience, one reflection)
   - 1 sample NightRunReport stored as a memory with tags ['night-run-report'] so the morning page has something to display
   The script should be runnable with: npx tsx scripts/seed-mock-data.ts

6. Cross-reference check:
   - Sidebar nav links match actual page routes
   - All API endpoints called from client components match actual API route files
   - Type imports are consistent between lib/types.ts, lib/ob1-api.ts, and components

7. Final build:
   - npm run build (must succeed)
   - Verify output shows all 9+ routes compiled
   - Start with npm run start and verify the login page loads at localhost:3000

If ANY issue is found, fix it. This task should end with a fully building, lint-clean dashboard.

Report: At the end, create a summary of what's working and any known limitations (e.g., 'Realtime requires Supabase anon key to be set', 'Morning report shows empty state until first night run').
